items.title: OpenAI gated GPT-5.6 behind federal vetting
items.oneliner: OpenAI previewed GPT-5.6 while Washington vets users first, turning frontier model access into a policy-controlled supply chain.

items.title: Anthropic released Mythos only to trusted institutions
items.oneliner: Anthropic's Mythos reached 100-plus US organizations, confirming frontier deployment now looks more like export control than SaaS.

items.title: DeepSeek published major inference speedups
items.oneliner: DeepSeek claims 60 to 85 percent faster generation, which matters more than benchmarks if you actually pay inference bills.

items.title: A suspected nation-state supply-chain attack failed
items.oneliner: A disguised repo attack slipped past obvious alarms, reminding teams that dependency trust is still the softest target.

items.title: AI security tooling missed a malicious package
items.oneliner: CVE-2026-LGTM showed AI review agents approving malware, which is exactly why automated AppSec still needs skeptical humans.

items.title: A new Apple SecureROM exploit landed
items.oneliner: usbliter8 targets A12 and A13 bootroms, proving low-level Apple attack surface still matters long after patchable software ends.

items.title: AWS added MicroVM sandboxes to Lambda
items.oneliner: AWS Lambda MicroVMs package Firecracker isolation as a service, making safer user-code execution finally look operationally sane.

items.title: Flink shipped a native S3 filesystem
items.oneliner: Flink 2.3's native S3 layer reportedly doubles checkpoint speed and cuts Hadoop baggage your security team never wanted.

items.title: devenv attacked Nix startup latency
items.oneliner: devenv traced Nix slowness to dynamic loader overhead, exposing how much developer time disappears before code even runs.

items.title: Apple blamed AI demand for hardware price hikes
items.oneliner: Apple says AI datacenter demand is pushing up memory and storage costs, so infrastructure inflation is now hitting end devices.
